input.title: Social Media Application
input.description: A mobile-first social networking platform where users create profiles, share text and image posts, follow other users, interact through likes and comments, exchange direct messages, and share ephemeral stories. The platform includes a personalized news feed, user discovery, content moderation tools, and granular privacy controls.

user_classes.User: Registered individual who creates content, interacts with other users, and manages their own profile and privacy settings.
user_classes.Admin: Platform administrator with full access to user management, content moderation dashboards, and platform configuration.
user_classes.Moderator: Staff member who reviews flagged content, issues warnings, and manages content visibility.

definitions.Feed: Personalized timeline of posts from followed users and suggested content, ordered by relevance and recency.
definitions.Story: Ephemeral image or short video post that disappears after 24 hours.
definitions.Thread: A direct message conversation between two users.
definitions.Engagement: User interactions with content including likes, comments, shares, and saves.

scope: User registration with email/phone and OAuth via Google and Apple
Profile creation and editing with avatar, bio, and links
Text and image/video post creation with captions and hashtags
Personalized news feed with infinite scroll
Like, comment, and share functionality on posts
Follow/unfollow system with follower and following counts
User search by name, username, and hashtag search
Content reporting, flagging, and admin moderation
Privacy settings for profile visibility and message permissions
out_of_scope: Algorithmic content recommendation using ML models in the first release
constraints: The mobile app must be built with React Native for cross-platform iOS and Android support
Firebase is used for authentication, real-time database for messaging, and cloud storage for media
Node.js backend handles business logic, feed generation, and moderation workflows
Image uploads must be compressed and resized server-side to reduce bandwidth usage
All media must be stored in Firebase Cloud Storage with CDN delivery

operating_environment.frontend: React Native application deployed to iOS App Store and Google Play Store.
operating_environment.backend: Node.js REST API deployed on Google Cloud Run. Firebase for auth, real-time messaging, and storage.
operating_environment.deployment: CI/CD via GitHub Actions. Backend deploys to Cloud Run. App builds via EAS Build for React Native.

FR-01.title: User Registration and Authentication
requirements: Users can register with email or phone number and a password
Email or phone verification is required before full account access
OAuth sign-in via Google and Apple is supported
Users must choose a unique username during registration
JWT-based session tokens are issued on login with automatic refresh
Account lockout occurs after 5 consecutive failed login attempts for 15 minutes

FR-02.title: User Profile
requirements: Users can set a profile picture, display name, bio (max 150 characters), and website link
Profile page shows the user's post count, follower count, and following count
Profile displays a grid of the user's posts in reverse chronological order
Users can switch between public and private account modes
Username changes are limited to once every 30 days
Verified badge is displayed for admin-verified accounts

FR-03.title: Post Creation and Sharing
requirements: Users can create text posts with a caption up to 2200 characters
Posts can include up to 10 images or a single video up to 60 seconds
Images are automatically compressed and resized to standard dimensions
Users can tag other users in posts and captions using @mentions
Posts can include hashtags that become searchable links
Users can edit post captions after publishing but cannot change media
Users can delete their own posts at any time

FR-04.title: News Feed
requirements: Feed displays posts from followed users sorted by a combination of recency and engagement
Feed loads incrementally with infinite scroll pagination
New posts from followed users appear at the top with a 'new posts available' indicator
Feed excludes posts from blocked users and muted accounts
Pull-to-refresh fetches the latest posts
Feed renders text, single image, image carousel, and video post types

FR-05.title: Likes and Comments
requirements: Users can like and unlike posts with a single tap
Like count is displayed on each post and updates in real time
Users can add text comments on posts
Comments support @mention tagging with autocomplete
Comment authors can delete their own comments
Post owners can delete any comment on their posts
Nested replies to comments are supported up to one level deep

FR-06.title: Follow and Unfollow
requirements: Users can follow and unfollow other users from their profile or posts
Following a private account sends a follow request that must be approved
Users can view their followers list and following list
Users can remove a follower from their followers list
Follow requests can be accepted or declined from a pending requests screen
Follower and following counts update in real time on profiles

FR-07.title: Direct Messaging
requirements: Users can send text messages to any user they follow or who follows them
Messages are delivered in real time using WebSocket or Firebase Realtime Database
Message threads show sender name, avatar, last message preview, and timestamp
Unread message count badge appears on the messages tab icon
Users can delete individual messages from their view
Typing indicator shows when the other user is composing a message
Users can share posts to other users via direct message

FR-08.title: Stories
requirements: Users can upload an image or short video as a story visible for 24 hours
Stories appear in a horizontal scrollable bar at the top of the feed
Tapping a user's avatar opens their story in a full-screen viewer with auto-advance
Story owners can see a list of users who viewed their story
Stories from followed users are displayed; stories from unfollowed users are hidden
Users can reply to a story which opens a direct message thread

FR-09.title: Search and Discovery
requirements: Users can search for other users by name or username with autocomplete suggestions
Hashtag search shows all public posts tagged with the searched hashtag
Explore page shows trending posts and popular hashtags
Recently searched users and hashtags are saved for quick access
Search results exclude blocked users and private accounts the user does not follow

FR-10.title: Push Notifications
requirements: Users receive push notifications for new followers, likes, comments, and mentions
Users receive push notifications for new direct messages
Users receive a notification when a follow request is approved
In-app notification feed shows all notifications with read/unread status
Users can configure which notification types they want to receive
Notification preferences are synced across devices

FR-11.title: Content Moderation
requirements: Users can report posts, comments, or profiles for violations
Reports include a reason category (spam, harassment, nudity, misinformation, other)
Moderators can review reported content and take action: approve, remove content, or warn user
Accounts accumulating 3 warnings are automatically suspended for review
Admin dashboard shows moderation queue, action history, and violation statistics
Removed content is replaced with a violation notice visible to the author

FR-12.title: Privacy Settings
requirements: Users can set their account to private, requiring follow approval for non-followers to see posts
Users can block other users, hiding all mutual content and preventing interactions
Users can mute other users, hiding their posts from the feed without unfollowing
Users can control who can send them direct messages: everyone, followers only, or no one
Users can hide their online status and last seen timestamp
Users can download a copy of all their data (profile, posts, messages) as a data export

NFR-01.title: Performance
requirements: News feed must load the first page within 2 seconds on a 4G connection
Image uploads must complete within 5 seconds for images up to 10MB
Direct messages must be delivered within 1 second in real time

NFR-02.title: Scalability
requirements: The platform must support 100,000 concurrent users without degradation
Feed generation must remain efficient as a user's follower graph grows to 10,000+

NFR-03.title: Security
requirements: All data in transit must be encrypted via HTTPS/TLS
User passwords must be hashed with bcrypt
Direct message content must be encrypted at rest
Authentication tokens must expire and rotate according to security best practices

system_attributes.reliability: Media uploads are resumable. Failed message deliveries are retried with exponential backoff.
system_attributes.scalability: Feed computation uses fan-out-on-read for users with few followers and fan-out-on-write for popular accounts.
system_attributes.security: Direct messages are encrypted at rest. OAuth tokens are stored securely in the device keychain.
system_attributes.maintainability: API versioning ensures backward compatibility. Feature flags control rollout of new features.

FR-01.title: Build Registration API with Email and Phone Verification
FR-01.description: Create POST /api/auth/register accepting email or phone, password, and chosen username. Validate username uniqueness and format (alphanumeric, underscores, 3-30 chars). Hash password with bcrypt. Send a 6-digit OTP via email or SMS for verification. Create POST /api/auth/verify-otp that confirms the code and activates the account. OTP expires after 10 minutes.
acceptance_criteria: Registration with duplicate username returns 409 with suggestion alternatives.
Registration with duplicate email/phone returns 409 without revealing which account owns it.
OTP is sent via email or SMS depending on the registration method.
Expired OTP returns 400 with option to resend.
Account is inactive until OTP verification is complete.

FR-01.title: Implement OAuth Login (Google and Apple)
FR-01.description: Implement Google Sign-In and Apple Sign-In flows for React Native. On successful OAuth, call POST /api/auth/oauth with the provider token. Backend verifies the token with the provider, creates or links an account using the provider email, and returns JWT access and refresh tokens. First-time OAuth users are prompted to choose a username before account creation is finalized.
acceptance_criteria: Google OAuth creates a new account or links to existing email-based account.
Apple OAuth handles the privacy relay email correctly.
First-time OAuth users must set a unique username before proceeding.
Returning OAuth users receive tokens immediately without re-registration.
Invalid or expired provider tokens return 401.

FR-01.title: Build Login Screen and Auth Flow UI
FR-01.description: Implement the React Native screens for login (email/phone + password), registration (email/phone + password + username), OTP verification (6-digit input with auto-focus and resend timer), Google and Apple sign-in buttons, and forgot password flow. Store tokens securely in device keychain. Implement automatic token refresh on 401 responses.
acceptance_criteria: Login form validates email/phone format and shows inline errors.
OTP screen auto-focuses the digit inputs and shows a countdown for resend.
Google and Apple buttons trigger the native OAuth flow on each platform.
Tokens are stored in secure keychain storage, not AsyncStorage.
Automatic token refresh retries the failed request transparently.

FR-01.title: Implement Account Lockout and Rate Limiting
FR-01.description: Track failed login attempts per account. After 5 consecutive failures within 30 minutes, lock the account for 15 minutes. Return a 429 response with the lockout duration. Implement IP-based rate limiting on auth endpoints (30 requests per minute). Send an email/SMS alert to the user when their account is locked.
acceptance_criteria: 6th failed login attempt returns 429 with remaining lockout time.
Successful login resets the failure counter.
Lockout automatically expires after 15 minutes.
User receives a security alert notification when account is locked.
IP-based rate limiting blocks excessive requests from a single IP.

FR-01.title: Create Users and Auth Schema
FR-01.description: Define the Firestore collections and document schema: users (uid, username, email, phone, passwordHash, displayName, avatarUrl, bio, websiteLink, isPrivate, isVerified, createdAt, updatedAt), auth_providers (userId, provider, providerUid), login_attempts (userId, attempts, lockedUntil). Create indexes for username lookup, email lookup, and phone lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Username field has a unique constraint enforced at the application level.
Email and phone fields are indexed for fast login lookups.
Auth provider documents link OAuth accounts to user records.
Login attempts documents support TTL for automatic cleanup.

FR-01.title: Write Authentication Module Tests
FR-01.description: Test registration with email and phone, duplicate username rejection, OTP verification (valid, expired, wrong code), login with correct and wrong credentials, account lockout after 5 failures, lockout expiry, Google and Apple OAuth account creation and linking, token refresh, and rate limiting.
acceptance_criteria: Registration tests cover email, phone, and OAuth pathways.
OTP tests verify valid, expired, and incorrect code scenarios.
Lockout tests confirm activation after 5 failures and automatic expiry.
Token refresh tests verify transparent retry on expired access tokens.

FR-02.title: Build Profile API Endpoints
FR-02.description: Create GET /api/users/:username that returns the public profile (displayName, avatar, bio, link, postCount, followerCount, followingCount, isPrivate, isVerified) plus the relationship status (following, followed_by, requested, blocked, none) relative to the authenticated viewer. Create PATCH /api/users/me for updating displayName, bio, websiteLink, and isPrivate. Enforce username change limit of once per 30 days via lastUsernameChange timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Public profile returns correct post, follower, and following counts.
Relationship status is accurate for all states (following, blocked, none, etc.).
Private account profiles hide the post grid for non-followers.
Username change within 30 days of last change returns 400 with the next allowed date.
Bio longer than 150 characters is rejected with 400.

FR-02.title: Build Profile Avatar Upload
FR-02.description: Create POST /api/users/me/avatar that accepts an image file, validates format (JPEG, PNG, WebP) and size (max 5MB), resizes to 400x400px and 150x150px thumbnails, uploads to Firebase Cloud Storage, and updates the user's avatarUrl. Delete the previous avatar from storage when a new one is uploaded.
acceptance_criteria: Avatar is resized to two dimensions (400px and 150px) and stored as WebP.
Invalid file formats return 400 with supported formats listed.
Files over 5MB return 413.
Previous avatar is deleted from storage to avoid orphaned files.
Avatar URL is updated on the user document and returned in the response.

FR-02.title: Build Profile Screen UI
FR-02.description: Implement the React Native profile screen showing avatar (tappable to view full-size or change if own profile), display name, username, bio, website link (tappable), stats row (posts, followers, following as tappable numbers), follow/unfollow/requested button for other users, and a post grid below. For own profile, show an Edit Profile button that opens the edit form. Implement pull-to-refresh and load-more for the post grid.
acceptance_criteria: Own profile shows Edit Profile button; others show Follow/Unfollow/Requested.
Tapping follower/following counts navigates to the respective list.
Post grid loads paginated with placeholder shimmer during loading.
Private profile of non-followed user shows a lock icon instead of posts.
Edit profile form pre-fills current values and validates on submit.

FR-02.title: Write Profile Module Tests
FR-02.description: Test profile retrieval for own profile, public user, private user (followed vs. not followed), blocked user. Test profile update with valid data, bio over 150 chars, username change cooldown. Test avatar upload with valid image, invalid format, oversized file, and storage cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Private profile correctly hides posts for non-followers.
Blocked user profiles return 404 instead of 403.
Avatar upload tests verify image processing and storage.
Username change cooldown is enforced correctly.

FR-03.title: Build Post Creation API
FR-03.description: Create POST /api/posts accepting caption (max 2200 chars), media files (up to 10 images or 1 video up to 60s), and tagged usernames. Process images: compress to 80% quality, resize to max 1080px width, generate thumbnail. Process video: transcode to H.264 720p if needed that needs to be max 60 seconds. Extract hashtags from caption and store as searchable tags. Store media in Firebase Cloud Storage and create the post document with userId, caption, mediaUrls, hashtags, mentionedUsers, createdAt.
acceptance_criteria: Post with valid caption and images is created and returns the post object with media URLs.
Caption over 2200 characters returns 400.
More than 10 images in a single post returns 400.
Video longer than 60 seconds returns 400.
Hashtags are extracted from caption and stored as a searchable array.
@mentioned users are validated and stored as references.

FR-03.title: Build Post Edit and Delete API
FR-03.description: Create PATCH /api/posts/:postId that allows the author to update the caption only (media cannot be changed). Re-extract hashtags and mentions from the updated caption. Create DELETE /api/posts/:postId that removes the post document, deletes all associated media from Cloud Storage, and removes associated likes, comments, and notifications. Only the post author can edit or delete.
acceptance_criteria: Caption edit updates hashtags and mentions correctly.
Non-author edit/delete attempts return 403.
Delete removes the post, its media files, all comments, and all likes.
Edit records the updatedAt timestamp.

FR-03.title: Build Post Creation UI with Media Picker
FR-03.description: Implement the React Native post creation screen with a multi-line text input for caption (character counter showing remaining), media picker supporting camera and gallery selection, multi-image selector with reorder and remove, video recorder with 60-second timer, and preview carousel showing selected media. Show inline hashtag highlighting in the caption as the user types. Add a tag users button that opens a searchable user list.
acceptance_criteria: Media picker allows selecting up to 10 images or 1 video from gallery or camera.
Selected images show in a reorderable preview carousel with remove buttons.
Character counter turns red when approaching or exceeding the 2200-character limit.
Hashtags are visually highlighted in the caption text input.
Upload progress indicators show per-file progress during submission.

FR-03.title: Implement Media Processing Pipeline
FR-03.description: Build a server-side media processing service that runs after upload: for images — validate format, strip EXIF data (except orientation), compress to 80% WebP quality, resize to 1080px max width, generate a 300px thumbnail. For videos — validate duration (max 60s) and format, transcode to H.264 720p MP4 if needed, extract a poster frame. Store processed files in Firebase Cloud Storage with CDN-friendly paths.
acceptance_criteria: Images are stripped of EXIF data for privacy (GPS, camera info).
Processed images are stored as WebP with a 300px thumbnail variant.
Videos are transcoded to H.264 720p with a poster frame image.
Processing completes within 10 seconds for typical uploads.
Original files are deleted after processing to save storage costs.

FR-03.title: Write Post Creation and Media Tests
FR-03.description: Test post creation with text only, text + single image, text + multiple images, text + video, caption over limit, too many images, video over 60s. Test caption editing with hashtag re-extraction. Test deletion cascading (media, likes, comments). Test media processing: image compression, EXIF stripping, video transcoding, and thumbnail generation.
acceptance_criteria: All post type combinations create valid posts.
Validation tests reject oversized content with specific errors.
Deletion cascade tests verify all related data is cleaned up.
Media processing tests verify output dimensions and format.

FR-04.title: Build Feed Generation API
FR-04.description: Create GET /api/feed that returns the authenticated user's personalized feed. Fetch posts from followed users, score each post by combining recency (time decay) and engagement (likes + comments weighted), sort by score descending, and return paginated results (20 per page) with cursor-based pagination. Exclude posts from blocked and muted users. Include the viewer's like status on each post. Support a 'new posts available' check via GET /api/feed/updates?since=timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Feed returns posts from followed users only (excluding blocked and muted).
Posts are ranked by a combination of recency and engagement score.
Cursor-based pagination returns consistent results without duplicates.
Each post includes the viewer's like status (liked: true/false).
Feed updates endpoint returns a count of new posts since the given timestamp.
Feed for a user following 1000 accounts returns within 500ms.

FR-04.title: Build Feed Screen UI
FR-04.description: Implement the React Native feed screen with an infinite-scroll FlatList rendering post cards. Each card shows: author avatar and username (tappable to profile), post media (single image, carousel with dots indicator, or video with play controls), caption with expandable 'more' link, like button with animation, comment count link, share button, and relative timestamp. Add a pull-to-refresh gesture and a 'New posts' floating button that scrolls to top when tapped.
acceptance_criteria: Feed loads first page and renders posts within 2 seconds.
Infinite scroll fetches the next page when scrolling near the bottom.
Image carousels swipe horizontally with page dots indicator.
Videos autoplay muted when in viewport and pause when scrolled away.
Pull-to-refresh fetches latest posts and shows a loading spinner.
'New posts' button appears when updates are available and scrolls to top.

FR-04.title: Write Feed Generation Tests
FR-04.description: Test feed returns only followed users' posts, excludes blocked users, excludes muted users, rankings by recency and engagement, pagination consistency across multiple pages, empty feed for user following nobody, feed updates endpoint returns correct count, and feed performance with large follower graphs (1000+ following).
acceptance_criteria: Feed correctly excludes posts from blocked and muted users.
Pagination test fetches 3 consecutive pages without duplicates or gaps.
Ranking test verifies higher-engagement recent posts appear before older ones.
Performance test confirms sub-500ms response for users following 1000 accounts.

FR-05.title: Build Like and Unlike API
FR-05.description: Create POST /api/posts/:postId/like that adds a like record (userId, postId, createdAt) and increments the post's likeCount atomically. Create DELETE /api/posts/:postId/like that removes the like and decrements the count. Prevent duplicate likes (idempotent). Trigger a push notification to the post author (unless self-like). Create GET /api/posts/:postId/likes that returns a paginated list of users who liked the post.
acceptance_criteria: Liking a post increments likeCount atomically and returns the updated count.
Liking an already-liked post is idempotent and returns 200.
Unlike decrements the count and removes the like record.
Post author receives a notification on like (not for self-likes).
Likes list returns users with avatar and username, paginated.

FR-05.title: Build Comment System API
FR-05.description: Create POST /api/posts/:postId/comments accepting text (max 1000 chars) and optional parentCommentId for replies (max 1 level). Extract @mentions from comment text. Create GET /api/posts/:postId/comments returning top-level comments with nested replies, paginated. Create DELETE /api/posts/:postId/comments/:commentId allowing deletion by comment author or post owner. Trigger notifications for comment and mention.
acceptance_criteria: Comments are created with the correct post and author references.
Replies are nested under parent comments (max 1 level deep).
Replying to a reply attaches to the same parent (no deeper nesting).
Comment deletion by author or post owner returns 200; others get 403.
@mentioned users receive notifications with a link to the comment.
Comment count on the post updates atomically.

FR-05.title: Build Like Animation and Comments UI
FR-05.description: Implement the double-tap-to-like gesture on post images with a heart animation overlay. Build the like button with filled/outlined heart toggle and count display. Build the comments bottom sheet with top-level comments and expandable replies. Comment input bar with @mention autocomplete that searches followed users. Swipe-to-delete for own comments. Long-press menu for post owners to delete any comment.
acceptance_criteria: Double-tap on image shows a heart animation and likes the post.
Like button toggles between filled and outlined with count update.
Comments bottom sheet shows threaded replies with 'View replies' expand.
@mention autocomplete shows matching usernames from followed users.
Swipe-to-delete reveals a delete button on own comments.

FR-05.title: Write Likes and Comments Tests
FR-05.description: Test like, unlike, duplicate like idempotency, like count accuracy, likes list pagination. Test comment creation, reply nesting (1 level max), @mention extraction, deletion by author, deletion by post owner, deletion rejection for other users, comment count accuracy, and notification triggering for comments and mentions.
acceptance_criteria: Like count tests verify atomic increment/decrement under concurrent likes.
Comment nesting tests confirm max 1-level depth enforcement.
Deletion permission tests verify author and post owner access.
Notification tests confirm correct triggers for likes, comments, and mentions.

FR-06.title: Build Follow System API
FR-06.description: Create POST /api/users/:userId/follow that creates a follow relationship or a pending follow request (for private accounts). Create DELETE /api/users/:userId/follow to unfollow. Create POST /api/follow-requests/:requestId/accept and /reject for handling pending requests. Create DELETE /api/users/:userId/followers to remove a follower. Create GET /api/users/:userId/followers and /following with paginated user lists. Update follower/following counts atomically.
acceptance_criteria: Following a public account creates an immediate follow relationship.
Following a private account creates a pending request instead.
Accepting a follow request creates the relationship and updates counts.
Rejecting a request deletes it without notification.
Removing a follower decrements counts and deletes the relationship.
Follower and following counts are atomically consistent.

FR-06.title: Build Follow UI Components
FR-06.description: Implement the Follow/Unfollow/Requested button component that shows the correct state based on the relationship. Build the followers and following list screens with user cards (avatar, name, username, follow-back button). Build the follow requests screen showing pending requests with accept/decline buttons. Add follow/unfollow actions on post feeds and user search results.
acceptance_criteria: Button shows 'Follow', 'Following' (with unfollow on long-press), or 'Requested' based on state.
Followers list shows a 'Remove' button next to each follower on own profile.
Follow requests screen shows pending requests with accept/decline actions.
Following/unfollowing from feed or search updates the button state immediately.

FR-06.title: Write Follow System Tests
FR-06.description: Test follow public user, follow private user (request flow), accept/reject request, unfollow, remove follower, follower/following count accuracy, prevent self-follow, prevent following blocked users, follower list pagination, and concurrent follow/unfollow race conditions.
acceptance_criteria: Public and private follow flows are tested separately.
Self-follow and blocked-user follow are rejected.
Count accuracy tests verify atomic updates under concurrent operations.
Follow request accept/reject tests verify correct state transitions.

FR-07.title: Build Real-Time Messaging Backend
FR-07.description: Implement direct messaging using Firebase Realtime Database for real-time delivery. Create message thread documents (participants, lastMessage, lastTimestamp, unreadCounts). Create POST /api/messages/threads to initiate a thread (validate messaging permissions based on follow relationships). Messages within threads are stored as sub-documents with senderId, text, createdAt, readAt. Implement real-time listeners for new messages and read receipts. POST /api/messages/threads/:threadId/messages to send a message.
acceptance_criteria: Messages are delivered in real time via Firebase listeners.
Thread creation enforces message permission settings (follow-based).
Unread count per thread updates when messages arrive and resets on read.
Messages include sender info and timestamp.
Sending to a blocked user returns 403.

FR-07.title: Build Messaging UI
FR-07.description: Implement the React Native messaging screens: threads list showing each conversation with the other user's avatar, name, last message preview, timestamp, and unread badge. Thread detail screen with message bubbles (sent right in blue, received left in gray), timestamps on time gaps, typing indicator at the bottom, and text input with send button. Implement post sharing via DM — when a user taps 'Share' on a post, open a user picker and send the post as a message card.
acceptance_criteria: Thread list sorts by most recent message and shows unread badges.
Messages appear in real time without manual refresh.
Typing indicator shows when the other user is composing.
Shared posts render as tappable cards within the message thread.
Unread badge on the messages tab icon reflects total unread count.

FR-07.title: Implement Message Encryption at Rest
FR-07.description: Encrypt message content before storing in Firebase Realtime Database using AES-256 encryption with per-thread encryption keys. Store encryption keys in a secure server-side key store (not in the client). Decrypt messages server-side before delivery to authenticated thread participants. Ensure deleted messages have their ciphertext removed from storage.
acceptance_criteria: Message content is stored as ciphertext in Firebase, not plain text.
Encryption keys are stored server-side and never sent to the client.
Only authenticated thread participants can decrypt and read messages.
Deleted messages have ciphertext fully removed from storage.

FR-07.title: Write Messaging Module Tests
FR-07.description: Test thread creation between mutual followers, thread creation rejection when messaging is disabled by recipient, message sending and real-time delivery, unread count updates, message deletion, typing indicator events, post sharing via DM, message encryption (verify stored data is not plaintext), and messaging blocked users.
acceptance_criteria: Permission tests verify messaging restrictions based on follow and settings.
Real-time delivery test confirms messages appear on both sides.
Encryption test verifies Firebase stored data is not readable plaintext.
Blocked user messaging returns appropriate error.

FR-08.title: Build Stories API
FR-08.description: Create POST /api/stories accepting an image or video (max 15 seconds). Process media with the same pipeline as posts. Store with a 24-hour TTL. Create GET /api/stories/feed that returns stories from followed users grouped by user, sorted by most recently updated. Create GET /api/stories/:storyId/viewers that returns the list of users who viewed the story (only visible to the author). Create POST /api/stories/:storyId/view to record a view. Implement a scheduled cleanup job that deletes expired stories and their media.
acceptance_criteria: Stories expire exactly 24 hours after creation.
Stories feed groups by user and shows users with unseen stories first.
View tracking records unique viewers (no duplicate counts).
Viewer list is only accessible to the story author.
Expired stories and their media are cleaned up by the scheduled job.
Story replies create a DM thread with the story referenced.

FR-08.title: Build Stories UI
FR-08.description: Implement the stories bar at the top of the feed showing followed users' avatars in circles with a gradient ring for unseen stories and a gray ring for seen. Tapping opens a full-screen story viewer with auto-advance (5 seconds for images), progress bars at the top, tap-left/tap-right for prev/next, and swipe-down to dismiss. Show the viewer count at the bottom for own stories. Add a reply input that opens a DM to the story author. Build the story camera screen with image capture and 15-second video recording.
acceptance_criteria: Stories bar shows gradient ring for unseen and gray for seen stories.
Full-screen viewer auto-advances with progress bars and supports tap navigation.
Own stories show view count, tappable to see viewer list.
Reply input is visible at the bottom of other users' stories.
Story camera supports photo and timed video recording (max 15s).

FR-08.title: Write Stories Module Tests
FR-08.description: Test story creation with image and video, 24-hour expiry enforcement, stories feed grouping and ordering (unseen first), view tracking (unique per user), viewer list access control (author only), story reply creating DM thread, expired story cleanup job, and stories from unfollowed/blocked users being excluded.
acceptance_criteria: Expiry test confirms story is not returned in feed after 24 hours.
View tracking prevents duplicate views from the same user.
Feed ordering places users with unseen stories before seen ones.
Cleanup job removes expired stories and associated media files.

FR-09.title: Build Search API
FR-09.description: Create GET /api/search/users?q=query that searches users by display name and username with prefix matching and returns results ranked by follower count. Create GET /api/search/hashtags?q=query that searches hashtags by prefix and returns results ranked by post count. Create GET /api/explore/trending that returns the top 20 trending hashtags (highest post count in last 24 hours) and top trending posts (highest engagement in last 24 hours). Exclude blocked users and private accounts from search results.
acceptance_criteria: User search returns matches by name and username, ranked by follower count.
Hashtag search returns matching hashtags with post counts.
Trending hashtags are calculated from the last 24 hours of posts.
Blocked users and non-followed private accounts are excluded from results.
Search results return within 300ms for the general user base.

FR-09.title: Build Search and Explore UI
FR-09.description: Implement the React Native search screen with a text input that shows autocomplete results as the user types (debounced at 300ms). Show tabs for Users and Hashtags in results. Build the explore page with a trending hashtags section and a grid of trending posts. Implement recent searches with clear all button stored locally. Tapping a hashtag navigates to a page showing all posts with that hashtag.
acceptance_criteria: Search autocomplete triggers after 300ms debounce with results.
Users tab shows avatar, display name, username, and follow status.
Hashtags tab shows hashtag name and post count.
Explore page renders trending hashtags as tappable chips and trending posts in a grid.
Recent searches persist across app restarts and can be cleared.

FR-09.title: Write Search and Discovery Tests
FR-09.description: Test user search by name and username, hashtag search by prefix, trending calculation over 24-hour window, exclusion of blocked and private users from results, empty search results, and search performance under load.
acceptance_criteria: User search matches partial name and username correctly.
Trending hashtags reflect the last 24 hours only, not all time.
Blocked and private user exclusions work correctly in results.
Search responds within 300ms for the expected database size.

FR-10.title: Build Push Notification Service
FR-10.description: Implement a notification service that sends push notifications via Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM). Create notification triggers for: new follower, like on post, comment on post, @mention in comment, new DM, follow request accepted. Store device FCM tokens per user (support multiple devices). Queue notifications and batch-send to avoid rate limits. Build GET /api/notifications returning the in-app notification feed with read/unread status and PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read to mark as read.
acceptance_criteria: Push notifications are delivered to all registered devices for the user.
Each notification type has a specific title and body format.
In-app notification feed returns notifications sorted by newest with read/unread status.
Marking a notification as read updates the unread count.
Notifications are not sent for actions by blocked or muted users.

FR-10.title: Build Notification Preferences API
FR-10.description: Create GET/PATCH /api/users/me/notification-preferences that allows users to enable or disable each notification type: likes, comments, mentions, followers, follow_requests, direct_messages. Store preferences in the user document. The notification service checks preferences before sending. Preferences sync across devices via the API.
acceptance_criteria: Users can disable specific notification types independently.
Disabled notification types are not sent via push or stored in the feed.
Default preferences enable all notification types for new users.
Preferences persist and sync across multiple devices.

FR-10.title: Build Notifications UI
FR-10.description: Implement the React Native notifications screen showing a chronological feed of notifications grouped by today, this week, and earlier. Each notification shows the actor's avatar, action description, and timestamp. Unread notifications have a visual indicator (dot or highlight). Tapping a notification navigates to the relevant content (post, profile, or message). Show an unread badge on the tab bar icon. Build the notification preferences screen with toggles for each notification type.
acceptance_criteria: Notifications are grouped by time period (today, this week, earlier).
Unread notifications are visually distinct from read ones.
Tapping a notification navigates to the correct screen.
Tab badge shows the total unread count and updates in real time.
Preferences screen toggles persist and take effect immediately.

FR-10.title: Configure Firebase Cloud Messaging Integration
FR-10.description: Set up FCM for both iOS and Android in the React Native app. Configure APNs certificates for iOS push delivery. Implement device token registration on app launch and token refresh. Handle background and foreground notification display. Configure notification channels on Android for different notification types.
acceptance_criteria: Push notifications are delivered on both iOS and Android devices.
APNs certificate is configured for iOS production and sandbox.
Device tokens are registered on app launch and updated on refresh.
Foreground notifications show as in-app banners, not system notifications.
Android notification channels allow users to control types at the OS level.

FR-10.title: Write Notification Module Tests
FR-10.description: Test notification triggers for all event types (follow, like, comment, mention, DM, request accepted). Test preference enforcement — disabled types should not generate notifications. Test multi-device delivery. Test notification feed pagination and read/unread status. Test that actions from blocked/muted users do not trigger notifications.
acceptance_criteria: Each event type correctly triggers the corresponding notification.
Disabled notification types are suppressed in push and in-app feed.
Multi-device test confirms delivery to all registered tokens.
Blocked/muted user actions do not generate notifications.

FR-11.title: Build Content Reporting API
FR-11.description: Create POST /api/reports accepting targetType (post, comment, user), targetId, and reason (spam, harassment, nudity, misinformation, other). Create the report record with reporter, target, reason, status (pending), and timestamp. Prevent duplicate reports from the same user for the same target. Create GET /api/admin/reports with filtering by status, reason, and target type, sorted by newest first. Create PATCH /api/admin/reports/:reportId with action (dismiss, remove_content, warn_user, suspend_user).
acceptance_criteria: Reports are created with correct target references and reason.
Duplicate reports from the same reporter for the same target return 409.
Admin reports endpoint supports filtering by status, reason, and type.
Remove action hides the content and replaces it with a violation notice.
Warn action increments the user's warning count; 3 warnings trigger suspension.
Suspended accounts cannot log in and see a suspension message.

FR-11.title: Build Admin Moderation Dashboard
FR-11.description: Build a web-based admin panel (Next.js or React) showing the moderation queue: list of pending reports with content preview, reporter info, and reason. Action buttons for dismiss, remove content, warn user, and suspend user. Moderation history view showing all past actions with moderator name and timestamp. Statistics dashboard showing report volume by reason, average resolution time, and active violations.
acceptance_criteria: Moderation queue shows pending reports with content preview and reporter details.
One-click actions (dismiss, remove, warn, suspend) update the report immediately.
Action history is filterable by moderator, action type, and date range.
Statistics dashboard shows charts for report volume and resolution metrics.

FR-11.title: Write Content Moderation Tests
FR-11.description: Test report creation for posts, comments, and users. Test duplicate report prevention. Test admin actions: dismiss (report resolved, content stays), remove (content hidden, violation notice shown), warn (warning count incremented), suspend (account locked at 3 warnings). Test that suspended users cannot log in. Test moderation queue filtering and pagination.
acceptance_criteria: All report target types (post, comment, user) are tested.
Three-warning suspension is triggered automatically.
Removed content shows violation notice to the author.
Suspended account login returns 403 with suspension message.

FR-12.title: Build Privacy Controls API
FR-12.description: Create PATCH /api/users/me/privacy for updating privacy settings: isPrivate (public/private toggle), messagePermission (everyone/followers/none), showOnlineStatus, showLastSeen. Create POST /api/users/:userId/block and DELETE /api/users/:userId/block for blocking/unblocking. Blocking removes the follow relationship in both directions, hides all mutual content, and prevents all interactions. Create POST /api/users/:userId/mute and DELETE /api/users/:userId/mute for muting/unmuting (hides posts from feed without unfollowing).
acceptance_criteria: Setting account to private immediately requires follow approval for new followers.
Blocking removes mutual follows, hides content, and prevents search discovery.
Unblocking does not restore the previous follow relationship.
Muting hides posts from feed without changing the follow state.
Message permission 'none' prevents all incoming DM thread creation.
Online status and last seen are hidden when the respective setting is off.

FR-12.title: Build Data Export API
FR-12.description: Create POST /api/users/me/data-export that queues a background job to compile all user data: profile info, all posts with media URLs, all comments, direct message history, followers and following lists, and settings. Package as a ZIP file, store temporarily in Cloud Storage, and email the user a download link valid for 48 hours. Limit export requests to once per 7 days.
acceptance_criteria: Export includes profile, posts, comments, messages, and relationships.
ZIP file is generated asynchronously without blocking the API.
Download link is emailed to the user and expires after 48 hours.
Repeated export requests within 7 days return 429 with next allowed date.
Temporary export files are cleaned up after link expiry.

FR-12.title: Build Privacy Settings UI
FR-12.description: Implement the React Native privacy settings screen with toggles for: private account, online status, last seen, and message permissions (segmented control: everyone/followers/none). Build the blocked users list with unblock buttons. Build the muted users list with unmute buttons. Add a data export request button with a confirmation dialog and a status indicator for pending exports.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy toggle changes take effect immediately via API call.
Blocked and muted user lists load with pagination.
Unblock and unmute actions update the list immediately.
Data export button shows a confirmation and then a pending status.
Message permission segmented control correctly reflects and updates the setting.

FR-12.title: Write Privacy and Security Tests
FR-12.description: Test private account toggle and its effect on follow behavior. Test block/unblock: mutual follow removal, content hiding, search exclusion, interaction prevention. Test mute/unmute: feed exclusion without follow change. Test message permissions enforcement. Test data export generation, download link expiry, and rate limiting. Test online status and last seen hiding.
acceptance_criteria: Block tests verify all side effects: follow removal, content hiding, search exclusion.
Mute tests verify feed exclusion without unfollowing.
Message permission tests verify enforcement for each setting level.
Data export tests verify ZIP content and download link lifecycle.
